results.title: Manager Data Governance job with Philip Morris International | 5540401
highlights:
Manager Data Governance job with Philip Morris International | 5540401
...
Employer Philip Morris International Location Madrid, Spain Salary Competitive Closing date 27 Jul 2026
...
This role is being evaluated as SG12One clear purpose - to deliver a smoke-free future.Times are changing at PMI. We've chosen to do something big. The world expects us to act responsibly. And we are doing just that by transforming our business by building a smoke-free future.With huge change comes huge opportunity. Wherever you join us, you'll enjoy the freedom to deliver better, brighter solutions and the space to move your career forward in endlessly different directions.As PMI accelerates its transformation, data has become a critical strategic asset. Reliable, trusted, and well-governed data is essential to support decision-making, advanced analytics, and scalable AI solutions.Our GTR data organization is strengthening its Data Governance & Engineering capability, with end-to-end ownership of governed data delivery across Bronze (raw and staging), Silver (refined), and Gold (curated and consumption-ready) layers, ensuring data is analytics and AI ready.Purpose of the jobAs a Manager Data Governance, you will lead the GTR data governance and engineering vertical with end-to-end ownership of governed data delivery from Landing to Presentation Stages. The role combines governance responsibilities, including standards, stewardship, quality, metadata, lineage, and access, with engineering delivery of pipelines, datasets, and semantic models. You will manage a mixed team and partner closely with business and IT stakeholders to improve data integrity, accessibility, and reuse across multiple countries.Key responsibilitiesYou will define and operate the GTR data governance framework aligned with global requirements, including policies, standards, roles, and decision forums.You will own data domain onboarding, defining critical data elements, data ownership, stewardship workflows, and escalation paths for data issues.You will lead and develop the Data Governance & Engineering team by setting clear objectives, prioritizing delivery, and coaching team members.You will establish and run data quality controls, monitoring, dashboards, and remediation workflows, and perform periodic assessments.You will define and enforce data access, classification, retention, and confidentiality requirements in partnership with security, compliance, and platform teams.You will oversee metadata management, catalog adoption, business glossary, and lineage using Atlan to drive transparency and reuse.You will own end-to-end delivery of trusted data products across Raw (landing), Staging (Harmonized) and Marts (Presentation) on Snowflake, ensuring performance, reliability, and analytics and AI readiness.You will define and track governance and engineering KPIs, and promote adoption through training, communication, and a strong data ownership culture.Required skills
...
- Strong experience in data governance, data quality management, metadata management, and stewardship operating models.
...
- Solid SQL and data modeling skills, including dimensional and semantic modeling, with experience validating analytics-ready datasets.
...
- Experience with data engineering and analytics engineering practices, including ELT/ETL patterns, orchestration, testing, and documentation.
...
- Hands-on
...
and collaborating with platform teams on performance and
...
- 7+ years' experience across data governance and data engineering/analytics engineering in a complex, multi-country/global enterprise environment.
...
Experience implementing data quality and observability tooling and integrating controls into pipelines (tests, monitors, alerts).
...
data catalog/
...
lineage rollout.
...
- Hands-on experience with Snowflake (preferred) and Atlan (preferred) in production, including governance integration (catalog/lineage) and secure data sharing/consumption patterns.

I recently had the privilege of participating as a presenter at the Philip Morris International AI - All IN event, where leaders across Europe came together to explore how AI will reshape the way we work, lead, and create value.
...
I presented the AI-Ready Data framework, built to ensure data is trusted so it can power AI use cases at scale. The motivation is clear: many AI initiatives struggle not because of algorithms, but because the underlying data is not ready. Inma Martinez , one of the invited presenters, reinforced a critical shift: moving from being AI-driven — where AI is bolted on top of existing processes — to becoming truly AI-native, where data, workflows, and decision-making are designed from the ground up around AI. For AI-Native organizations, Data Governance is not a constraint — it is an enabler of innovation. Some takeaways:
...
- Governance builds trust.
...
- By having clear, governed data pipelines, teams can experiment faster because they aren't spending 80% of their time "cleaning" or "verifying" the data.
...
- You must know exactly where a piece of data came from, who touched it, and how it was transformed before it hit the AI model.
...
- Especially in regulated sectors, being able to audit an AI’s logic is mandatory. Without governance, AI is a "black box," which she considers a massive business and social risk.
...
- As we move toward AI Agents, (AI that can take actions, not just answer questions), governance must evolve from Static to Dynamic: Traditional governance was about "data at rest" (databases). New governance must be about "data in motion"—monitoring how autonomous agents use and share data in real-time.
...
AI-ready data and strong governance are the bridge to an AI-native world.
...
Excited to turn these insights into real impact — one
...
dataset at a time. #AI #Data
...
#AIReadyData #AIGrowth #ALLIN
...
DataStrategy #
...
Transformation David Pass
...
Michał Piórkowski Reza Kar
...

highlights:

...
Leading strategic development and deployment of one of PMI’s flagship AI-powered platforms designed to transform decision-making across business domains. Helping business and regulatory strategy decisions by building fit for purpose data products. Truly enjoy building and packaging predictive/prescriptive models that leverage quant data into data products that help apply consumer and regulatory insights to a variety of digital/web, product innovation, acquisition & marketing strategies. Bringing experience with a variety of stakeholders x-cultures, respect, inclusion and strong inter personal skills to the table. Stakeholders see me as a key strategic partner to solve business problems.
...
Driving AI transformation by developing PMI’s AI-powered business oracle and semantic mining capabilities. Leading strategy, deployment, and enablement of the capabilities, helping teams make faster and smarter operational / strategic decisions and building an AI ready enterprise knowledge graph. Building structured semantics for the enterprise Enabling market-specific deployments and operational integration including user trainings. ...
Design & implementation of a suite of data products to facilitate insights generation using social media and digital data. Influenced multiple teams across various technical and business functions to deliver high quality results as the product owner. Distilled consumer and general public opinion into actionable topics/metrics (ontology/ KPI building) for various business functions such as Insights, Brand/Marketing, Regulatory Affairs, Scientific Communications, etc. Worked extensively with UI/UX designers to create intuitive and informative front ends. Built an always-on digital version of Brand Equity Advisor (BEA) yearly survey to track brand perception, importance and worth at industry level. Built an enterprise risk management data product that leverages public opinion and policy documents to monitor regulatory risk.
...
Built a holistic Consumer 360 View that draws upon layers of information. As a first step, developed a singular global consumer segmentation for audience targeting and marketing messaging. Followed by developing predictive models to increase retention (churn prevention) and eventually win-back consumers. Started with Italy as the pilot market and then went global with scope. Set and measured KPIs for consumer behavior across touchpoints (digital and physical consumer activity signals) Partnered with Product and User Experience teams to understand dogfooding device usage to segment consumers based on usage behaviour. Outlined possible pain points to impact future product development.

results.title: Tomás Jaramillo Quintero
highlights:
Sr. Manager Data, Insights & Analytics - CCA Cluster at [Philip Morris International](https://www.linkedin.com/company/insidepmi)
...
I'm a Senior Data & Analytics Manager at Philip Morris International, leading the data and AI agenda for the CCA cluster — 37 markets across Central America, Caribbean and Andean. Over the past 14 years I've worked across IT, CRM, and Data roles in Colombia, Canada, and Costa Rica, sitting at the intersection of business and engineering. Most of my work has been about turning ambiguity into shipped products: enterprise data platforms, predictive models in production, AI capability building, and the governance that makes any of it sustainable. Outside of work, I write at codebeats.net about the things I'm experimenting with — LLMs, autonomous agents, time-series foundation models, and the occasional side project that has nothing to do with any of that. Always happy to connect with people building in enterprise AI, data platforms, or AI-enabled products.
...
- Lead the data & analytics function for the CCA cluster (37 markets across Central America, Caribbean and Andean), managing a team of 15 across Data Foundation, BI, and Advanced Analytics & AI.
...
- Driving the AI agenda for the cluster: change management, governance, capability building, and shaping how AI fits within PMI's regulated context.
...
- Delivered cluster-wide talks on LLM fundamentals, frontier model developments, risk and regulation, real-world use cases at PMI, and Microsoft Copilot adoption.
...
- Leading the consolidation of the CCA Data Lake — prioritized ingestion roadmap, time-to-value optimization, and making the platform AI-ready through data homologation and governance. Projected annual savings of ~$260K.
...
- Partnering with the team on a portfolio of predictive models in production and pilot: demand forecasting (TFT), pricing simulation, Share of Market prediction, and customer churn — among others.
...
* Enable data products for commercial teams in CARICAM, leading a high-performance team to facilitate data-driven decision-making. * Collaborate with the IT team to enhance and upgrade our data platforms and infrastructure to the latest tech stack, optimizing for cost and developing machine learning/AI models to address business questions. * Act as a liaison between business stakeholders and IT teams, translating complex needs into clear business requirements. * Facilitate seamless product launches, drive digital evolution, and refine processes to contribute to operational excellence. * Play a key role in standardizing platforms across the CARICAM cluster, fostering improved collaboration and efficiency.
...

results.title: Philippe Linguri
highlights:
Belux Digital Transformation & AI Lead at [Philip Morris International](https://www.linkedin.com/company/insidepmi)
...
Most organizations have two problems they think are separate. They are not. The first is that information exists but does not move fast enough to drive decisions. The second is that their commercial teams operate on informal practices, inconsistent tools, and processes that made sense in a different era, and struggle to execute swiftly on prior decisions. Fix one without the other and you get beautiful dashboards nobody acts on, or an efficient machine running in random direction. I fix both at the same time. I do this by sitting at a place most organizations leave empty: between commercial, finance and IT. Between what the consultant deck says and what actually ships. I have spent twenty years bridging that gap: analytics foundations, commercial ecosystems, and now AI deployments, across telecom, FMCG, and enterprise consulting. What I bring to an organization is not only a methodology. It is a track record of making the pieces connect: information that nurtures educated decision-making and, as a result, improvements and value. Commercial processes that actually govern behavior, and technology that actually serves the people it was built for. The last part is harder than it sounds. Adoption is where most programs die. The frontier I am working on now is enterprise AI deployment: Microsoft Copilot & Cowork in back-office workflows, Salesforce Agentforce in front-office execution and not as a pilot but as an operating reality.
...
#### Belux Digital Transformation & AI Lead (Current)
...
i. Deploying AI across PM BeLux commercial operations — Microsoft Copilot in back-office workflows and Salesforce Agentforce in front-office execution — with 50+ active users and adoption programs expanding quarter on quarter. ii. Leading the digital transformation of Belgium & Luxembourg as a standalone commercial market, covering go-to-market evolution, operating model governance, and commercial technology integration in a regulated consumer goods environment. iii. Driving continuous evolution of the commercial KPI framework and analytics layer built in 2021 — ensuring that data infrastructure built for yesterday's operating model keeps pace with an AI-augmented one. iv. Designed PM Benelux's commercial-financial infrastructure from scratch: Salesforce, digital payments (Stripe, Trustly), PEPPOL e-invoicing, and automated KYC/KYB — cutting payment cycles from three weeks to a single day and removing the financial bottlenecks that had constrained commercial growth.
...
AI Champion by [Philip Morris International](https://linkedin.com/company/insidepmi)

## Drive innovation in machine learning and AI, from research to deployment, creating scalable intelligent systems that power the next generation of scientific discovery.

Are you passionate about turning complex data into intelligent, production-ready solutions while supporting the future of open-access science? We are looking for a Data Scientist to join our team and help design, develop, and deploy advanced machine learning solutions that power intelligent, data-driven products. This role focuses on NLP, recommender systems, and agentic LLM workflows, turning complex business challenges into scalable and impactful analytical solutions.

Monitor and analyze model performance and iterate to improve accuracy and robustness.
